name: Antwerp (Belgium)

colors.symbolism: from the colours of Antwerp city (red-white) and Turnhout (white-blue)
from the colours of the city of Turnhout (white-blue)
from the colours of the city of Mechelen (yellow-red)
from the colours of the city of Antwerp (red-white) and Mechelen (yellow-red)

description: A field of stepped diagonal bands running from the upper hoist toward the lower fly, in the order white, blue, white, yellow, white, red and white, in the chequered Brabant tradition.
history: Adopted by the Provincial Council on 18 October 1996 and approved by the Flemish Government on 7 January 1997, replacing a 1928–1997 yellow-red-white horizontal triband. The chequered Brabant tradition is honoured by combining the livery colours of the province's three principal cities: Antwerp (red-white), Mechelen (yellow-red) and Turnhout (white-blue).

features.symbolism: Stepped diagonal bands running from the upper hoist toward the lower fly: white, blue, white, yellow, white, red, white — the chequered Brabant pattern combining the livery colours of Antwerp (red-white), Mechelen (yellow-red) and Turnhout (white-blue).
